headline: Mustafizur struggled with line, issued multiple boundaries.
key_finding: A boundary rate allowed of 7% is well above the league average of 7.8%, indicating poor delivery location when under pressure. Play and miss rate of 31% suggests batsmen were comfortable laying off spin and slower variations.
concern_flag: High boundary rate in back-to-back matches warrants mechanical review.

headline: Rashid was untouchable — elite swing-and-miss stuff all night.
key_finding: Rashid generated a 37% play_and_miss rate, well above the league average of 25.4%. Velocity held steady above 94 kph through the 18th over with no signs of fatigue. Particularly dominant bowling to the outer third against right-handed batsmen.
pitch_mix_note: Pace-heavy mix (est. ~59%) with yorker as primary variation.

headline: Shaheen battled but relied on fielders for a solid outing.
key_finding: Velocity was down 1.9 kph from the 30-day baseline, though line and length compensated with a 53% stump line rate. expected_runs_per_ball allowed of 0.297 is near league average — no dominant delivery stood out today.
concern_flag: Velocity dip worth monitoring over the next two matches.

headline: Jasprit was sharp, controlling both sides of the crease effectively.
key_finding: A 32% Dot Ball Percentage reflects consistent zone presence combined with above-average chase rates. Jasprit's delivery mix diversity (entropy: 0.90) kept batsmen off-balance, contributing to soft contact when they did make contact.
pitch_mix_note: Pace-heavy mix (est. ~51%) with cutter as primary variation.
